Class Forest

java.lang.Object
jogo.models.Structures.Structures
jogo.models.Structures.Forest
public class Forest extends Structures
Represents a Forest structure in the game world. Forests act as production sites that generate wood resources for the owner while requiring stone for initial placement and maintenance expenses.
Author:
Fabio Cruz, Tiago Silva

    expense, level, MAINTENANCE_MATERIAL, owner, profit, UPGRADE_MATERIAL

    Forest(Player owner, int scoreModifier)
    Constructs a new Forest structure, initializing its base resource yields, cost constraints, and placing it under the specified player's ownership.

    void
    generateResource(int resourcesModifier)
    Executes the daily resource generation, adding the calculated amount of wood into the owner's inventory based on current profit and modifiers.

  • Constructor Details

    • Forest

      public Forest(Player owner, int scoreModifier)
      Constructs a new Forest structure, initializing its base resource yields, cost constraints, and placing it under the specified player's ownership.
      Parameters:
      owner - The Player who will own and maintain this forest.
      scoreModifier - The global score multiplier currently active.

      public boolean upgradeStructure(int scoreModifier) throws InsufficientResourcesException

      upgradeStructure in class Structures
      Throws:
      InsufficientResourcesException

    • generateResource

      public void generateResource(int resourcesModifier)
      Executes the daily resource generation, adding the calculated amount of wood into the owner's inventory based on current profit and modifiers.
      Specified by:
      generateResource in class Structures
      Parameters:
      resourcesModifier - The current active multiplier for resource production.
